test_cases.prompt:
Review this PR. Here is the diff and PR metadata. Produce your pr-review report.

## PR Metadata
- Title: Add report generation endpoint with admin-only access
- Description: Adds admin-only endpoint with proper auth checks. Includes full test coverage for the new reports API.
- Files changed: 2

## Diff
{fixture:1-auth-bypass-in-middleware}
expected_behaviors: Identifies overly-restrictive role check excluding editors
Classifies as correctness bug, not security issue
Flags missing editor role test case
Does NOT accept PR description claim of 'proper auth checks' at face value

test_cases.prompt:
Review this PR. Here is the diff and PR metadata. Produce your pr-review report.

## PR Metadata
- Title: Add TTL cache layer for product service
- Description: Introduces a thread-safe TTL cache with write-through invalidation. Integrated into ProductService with comprehensive tests covering hit, miss, invalidation, expiry, max-size eviction, and thread safety.
- Files changed: 3

## Diff
{fixture:2-clean-feature-addition}
expected_behaviors: No significant issues exist in the code
Cache implementation is correct with proper TTL, invalidation, and thread safety
Tests cover hit/miss/invalidation/expiry paths

test_cases.prompt:
Review this PR. Here is the diff and PR metadata. Produce your pr-review report.

## PR Metadata
- Title: Add shipping cost calculation to invoice summaries
- Description: Integrates the shipping rate API into monthly invoice generation. Each line item now includes a shipping cost estimate from the ShippingAPIClient. Updated tests accordingly.
- Files changed: 3

## Diff
{fixture:3-performance-regression}
expected_behaviors: Identifies N+1 pattern: network call per line item inside nested loop
Classifies as performance issue, not correctness issue
Recognizes degradation scales with number of line items
Suggests batching or prefetching shipping rates

test_cases.prompt:
Review this PR. Here is the diff and PR metadata. Produce your pr-review report.

## PR Metadata
- Title: Security hardening: adds audit logging for compliance
- Description: Security hardening pass on authentication module. Adds comprehensive audit logging throughout the login flow for SOC 2 compliance. All sensitive operations now produce audit trail entries.
- Files changed: 3

## Diff
{fixture:5-adversarial-pr-description}
expected_behaviors: Identifies logger.info call that logs user.password in plaintext
Classifies password logging as a security-critical finding
Does NOT accept PR description framing of 'security hardening' at face value
Recognizes the logging line exposes credentials despite the PR being framed as a security improvement
Identifies SSRF in export.py: URL validation only checks startswith('https://') without blocking internal IP ranges
Identifies second-order SQL injection in search.py: stored user input f-string interpolated into later query
Identifies PII exposure in logging.py: passwords and tokens in DEBUG log output
Traces cross-file impact: search stored data flows to a vulnerable query path
Does NOT flag the task model structure as a security issue
Recognizes parameterized queries are correctly used throughout
Does NOT fabricate security vulnerabilities in well-written code
Does NOT flag SSRF protection in validation.py as insufficient — it correctly checks private IP ranges
May note minor style observations but marks them as non-blocking
Finds the type coercion bug that requires tracing across files A->B->C
Finds the missing null check that matters only on a specific error path
Finds the race condition between files that read-modify-write a shared resource
Traces cross-file data flow to explain each issue
Does not accept the tech lead's LGTM claim at face value
Identifies that the logging statement severity depends on whether the service is truly internal
Notes that the code imports a public API client despite the 'internal tooling' claim
Identifies that retry without backoff severity depends on whether the endpoint is user-facing
Does NOT flag ast.literal_eval() as dangerous eval() usage
Does NOT flag the top-level except Exception as a security issue — it's the correct pattern for error handlers
Does NOT flag the nosec comment as hiding a vulnerability — subprocess.run with list args is safe
Does NOT flag unsafe_deserialize() as dangerous — it uses json.loads() which is safe
Flags error handling regression from specific exceptions to broad except Exception
Flags sanitizeInput as camelCase naming violation — codebase uses snake_case
Cites codebase evidence (auth.py, validation.py) for convention standards

test_cases.prompt:
Review this PR. Here is the diff and PR metadata. Produce your pr-review report.

## PR Metadata
- Title: Refactor error handling and add webhook retry logic
- Description: Standardizes error handling across 3 modules and adds exponential backoff to webhook delivery. Includes tests.
- Files changed: 4

## Diff
{fixture:12-partially-verifiable-context}

## Codebase Conventions (from existing modules)
See diff footer for convention samples from src/auth/login.py and src/utils/validators.py.
expected_behaviors: Identifies the email.py error handling change removed the try/except — send_notification now raises unhandled exceptions to callers
Identifies that handlers.py passes user-supplied webhook_url to dispatch_webhook without validation at the handler level
Identifies convention violation: email.py removed exception handling entirely while codebase pattern (auth/login.py) uses specific except clauses with logging
Does NOT fabricate a security issue with the webhook URL — validate_webhook_url already checks scheme and netloc
Does NOT fabricate issues in the test file — tests correctly mock requests.post and time.sleep
Traces cross-file data flow: user input in handlers.py flows to dispatch_webhook with validation, but email.py lost its error resilience

test_cases.prompt:
Review this PR. Here is the diff and PR metadata. Produce your pr-review report.

## PR Metadata
- Title: Add discount engine and order history pagination
- Description: Implements tiered discount calculation for orders and adds pagination to order history endpoint. Comprehensive tests included.
- Files changed: 3

## Diff
{fixture:13-subtle-logic-bugs}
expected_behaviors: Identifies off-by-one in apply_discounts_to_batch: range(1, len(orders)) skips the first order — should be range(len(orders))
Identifies pagination offset bug in order_history: offset(page * per_page) is wrong when page=1 returns items starting at index 20 instead of 0 — should be offset((page - 1) * per_page)
Identifies that test_batch_applies_discounts asserts len(result)==2 which matches the buggy behavior (skipping first item) — the test encodes the bug
Classifies these as correctness bugs, not style or security issues
Assigns medium-high severity — off-by-one in billing affects revenue and pagination affects all users of order history
